summary: Name resembles requests-toolbelt with empty payload
details: Package 'requests-toolbelt-plus' has a name closely resembling the widely-used 'requests-toolbelt' library and is published at an inflated version (99.9.10) with generic metadata (Summary: 'Extended HTTP library with additional features'; Author: 'Security Team'). The shipped module requests_toolbelt_plus/__init__.py is zero bytes, with no imports, network calls, subprocess use, or file I/O. There is no install-time or import-time behavior that would harm an installer, but the name-confusion risk against the legitimate package warrants human review to assess intent (placeholder squat vs. setup for a future malicious release).

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
